tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes. Brent at $106.40/bbl (Mar 9, +24.4%) — Iraq output down ~60% (Bloomberg, Mar 8); Kuwait cutting; JP Morgan projects 4.7M bpd GCC shut-in within 2 weeks. Goldman models $18/bbl Iran shock add-on; J.P. Morgan upper-end $130. Qatar loaded first cargo since force majeure but full restart contingent on conflict end. ASEAN energy producers (PTTEP, Medco Energi, Sapura Energy) benefit directly. Long near-dated Brent vs short deferred as backwardation steepens. Entered at $79; now $106.40; base target $115 (prior $106 HIT), stress target $135. Mojtaba Khamenei elected — no diplomatic off-ramp in sight.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: ASEAN Banks at 201.3 (-3.3% Mar 9) — selloff continues as credit widens; iTraxx Asia IG at 158bp (+13bp); ASEAN HY at 485bp (+25bp). ASEAN Energy Equities at 148.2 (+5.5%) bid on Iraq output collapse and Brent $106.40. Maintain bank long for rate-cut delay beneficiaries; reassess short energy exposure as Brent rally accelerates beyond base case. Wilmar and IJM plantation longs remain as oil-correlated ASEAN plays.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: PHP most vulnerable ASEAN currency per MUFG. USD/PHP at 59.23 (Mar 9, +0.2%) — approaching 59.50 strike breakeven. 2.2M+ OFWs in Gulf states; repatriation operations underway reduce remittance inflows. Philippines formally rejected EDCA-base link to Iran campaign. >50% of PHL power from imported fossil fuels; no strategic LNG reserves. Current account deficit widens to -4.5% GDP at Brent $106.40 — materially worse than modelled. BSP rate cut cycle on hold. MUFG stress target: 61.00. Stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 16,950 (Mar 9, -0.6%) — tactical IDR strength on commodity export inflows, but structural oil import burden overwhelming at Brent $106.40. Bank Indonesia rate hold at 4.75%; ID 10Y at 7.55% (+15bp). Iraq output -60% forces Indonesia to source dearer US/Australian crude. OFAC enforcement tightening on Indonesian-branded crude covering Iranian origin. GCC shut-in approaching 4.7M bpd compounds import costs. IDR tactical bounce does not invalidate structural short thesis. Target 17,500–18,000 in stress; 19,000 in tail.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 158bp (+13bp Mar 9 est.) — widening accelerates. Iraq output -60% + Kuwait cuts + ceasefire rejection by new Supreme Leader = triple-front credit deterioration. CDS entered at 102bp — now +56bp in-the-money. P&I blackout fully in effect; 329 tankers trapped. Hold existing 5Y position; 180–220bp credible stress target in 1–3 month disruption. Sapura Energy and Hyflux bonds most vulnerable ASEAN HY names. ASEAN HY at 485bp (+25bp est.) widening continues.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $15.50/MMBtu (Mar 9, -1.5%) — Qatar first cargo loaded since force majeure moderated near-term spike. TTF at €52.80/MWh (-4.7%). However: GCC crude supply collapse (4.7M bpd within 2 weeks per JP Morgan) creates LNG re-demand floor; Ras Laffan full restart contingent on war end. Reduce near-term JKM leg tactically; hold position for rebound as Iraqi oil loss translates to power-sector LNG demand. In 1–3 month extended closure, JKM could approach $22–25/MMBtu. IRGC Malacca threat remains extreme upside risk.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 face acute refinancing risk. iTraxx Asia IG at 158bp and ASEAN HY at 485bp — bank refinancing window now effectively closed. Iraq output -60%; Kuwait cutting; P&I blackout in effect. E&P revenue uplift at Brent $106.40 lags capex by 2–3 quarters. Shift immediately to private credit, DFI, or multilateral lenders — bilateral bank window has closed; iTraxx already through the 160bp threshold.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — pre-finance at current 3.38% TH 10Y before BOT inflation repricing accelerates. THB at 31.85; 3.60%+ plausible if Brent $106+ inflation persists. PTTEP upstream EBITDA materially improved at Brent $106.40 — use the revenue tailwind to justify pre-financing now. MAS FATF guidance requires Gulf of Oman JV compliance review. GCC supply collapse and Indian Ocean war expansion add LNG and crude offtake shipping risk.

Expand USD revolving credit facility immediately. Bunker fuel ~12% of cost base — at Brent $106.40, annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 est. USD 650–730M. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) improves EBITDA but cash conversion lag creates working capital pressure. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 750–1,000M recommended — iTraxx Asia IG at 158bp still executable for IG borrowers but window narrowing fast.

Base scenario now at 22% — Mojtaba Khamenei elected (Mar 8) and explicit ceasefire rejection reduce near-term de-escalation probability. At Brent $106.40 and ASEAN HY at 485bp, capital markets are closed for unsecured debt. Explore private credit, DFI, or multilateral bridge financing as only viable near-term path. Engage restructuring advisor immediately — energy cost covenant must be reviewed vs plan assumptions at $55–60/bbl. The $46+/bbl breach is structurally critical.
